message:
fix(window): fail-closed window-op futures instead of hanging on destroyed windows

Previously, when a window was destroyed between queuing a window
operation and its execution on the main thread, the returned future
would remain permanently pending. This caused silent hangs in callers
awaiting clipboard results or other window operations on closed panes.

This commit introduces fail-closed semantics across all platforms:

connection.rs:
- Add new_window_op_promise<T>() helper that creates a Promise/Future
  pair, centralizing the pattern used in every with_window_inner shim.
- Add fail_window_op_for_destroyed_window() helper that resolves the
  promise with a descriptive error when the target window no longer
  exists in the connection's window map.
- Add unit test verifying the fail-closed behavior (promise resolves
  to error, not pending).
- Fix indentation in log::error! formatting for display name fallback.

Platform shims (macOS, Wayland, Windows, X11):
- All with_window_inner() implementations now use new_window_op_promise()
  instead of manually constructing Promise + get_future().unwrap().
- All add an `else` branch that calls fail_window_op_for_destroyed_window()
  when the window ID is not found, ensuring the future resolves with an
  error rather than hanging.
- X11: the existing "window has been destroyed" check (when window_id
  doesn't match) now uses the shared helper for consistency.

Clipboard error propagation (Wayland + X11):
- In get_clipboard_data(), the future returned by with_window_inner is
  now captured and its error is forwarded into the outer clipboard
  promise via a spawned async task. Previously, if with_window_inner
  failed (destroyed window), the clipboard future would hang.
- The Promise is wrapped in Arc<Mutex<Option<Promise>>> to allow both
  the inner clipboard callback and the error-propagation task to
  access it safely.

Import reordering across all touched files follows rustfmt conventions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
